title: triticum caninum var. gracilius
intro: Triticum caninum var. gracilius, also known as the Wild Einkorn or slender dog's tail, is a rare, perennial grass that belongs to the wheat tribe. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to offer health benefits such as relief from digestive issues, fever reduction, and wound healing due to its bioactive compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids. It can be prepared for herbal use in various forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to fully understand its efficacy and potential side effects.
